lesson: An HTR SLOW cycle with a healthy heater drawing correct current means scale on the heat exchanger is throttling heat transfer. Descale it — do not swap the element.
symptoms: Heat-treat cycle taking twice as long as usual.
HTR SLOW alarm at the end of the cycle.

tests.error-log: HTR SLOW warnings logged for three weeks. Cycle completing but well outside spec time.
tests.temp-probe: Mix heats up but very slowly. Heater element draws correct current.
tests.inspect-beater: Internal heat exchanger surfaces visible through barrel access — coated in a 3–4 mm layer of white calcite scale.

flavour: Three weeks of slow baking on the company dime.
authenticityNote: Scale on heat exchanger surfaces reduces thermal transfer dramatically; the heater element is perfectly healthy but pushing heat through 4 mm of calcite takes twice as long. A descale run restores full performance.
